Philadelphia Greek Mob.description: A loose-knit Greek mafia faction consisting of Greek-American racketeers and drug dealers operating in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. Closely affiliated with the Italian-American Philadelphia crime family, it fell under complete control of that family during the reign of boss Nicky Scarfo. The group was involved in loansharking, extortion, methamphetamine trade, illegal gambling, and drug trafficking. Its leader, Chelsais 'Steve' Bouras, was assassinated in 1981, and the group's activity declined thereafter.

'Ndrina Zagari.description: The Zagari are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Taurianova, Italy, operating also in Northern Italy and Rome. They were involved in a violent feud with the Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi clans from the 1980s to the 1990s, resulting in 32 murders. The group is allied with the Viola clan and has been implicated in extortion, political corruption, and drug trafficking.

American Front.description: White supremacist organization founded in San Francisco, California by Bob Heick in 1984. Initially modeled after the British National Front, it evolved into a political group with skinhead and non-skinhead members. Active in multiple US states by 1989, it published the tabloid Aryan Warrior and held White Workers Day marches.

descriptions: American Front was modeled after the British National Front.

descriptions: American Front cooperated with White Aryan Resistance starting in 1988.

Sacra corona unita.description: Italian mafia-type criminal organization based in Apulia, primarily active in the Salento region. Founded in the early 1980s as a response to Camorra expansion, with ties to 'Ndrangheta. Reached its peak in the late 1980s and early 1990s, then weakened by state intervention. In the 2010s it adopted a strategy of infiltration into the business sector.

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia Salentina fought a feud against SCU's clans in Lecce.
edges.evidence_quote: Nuova Famiglia Salentina : formed in 1986 by De Matteis Pantaleo, from Lecce and stemming from the Famiglia Salentina Libera born in the early 1980s as an autonomous criminal movement in the Salento area with no links with extra-regional Mafia expressions, the organization fought a feud against SCU's clans in Lecce;

descriptions: SCU and NFL (formerly FSL) made peace in Lecce prison.
edges.evidence_quote: la pace siglata nel carcere di Lecce tra Pino Rogoli e la Famiglia Salentina Libera, guidata dal boss leccese Salvatore Rizzo e dal cognato Pantaleo De Matteis, che cambiò nome in Nuova Famiglia Salentina (NFL)

descriptions: Remo Lecce Libera is an antagonistic group that formed due to internal difficulties of SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Remo Lecce Libera : formed by some leading criminal figures from Lecce, who claim to be independent from any criminal group other than the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Rosa dei Venti is a splinter group that broke away from SCU due to conflicts with Rogoli.
edges.evidence_quote: La Rosa dei venti prese vita nel carcere di Lecce nel 1990 da Giovanni De Tommasi, Cosimo Cirfeta e Vincenzo Stranieri che richiesero il permesso alla 'Ndrangheta di staccarsi dalla Sacra Corona Unita per contrasti avuti con Rogoli

descriptions: Rosa dei Venti formed from an internal division in SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Rosa dei Venti : formed in 1990 by De Tommasi in the Lecce prison, following an internal division in the SCU.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera is a splinter group from SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Alcuni di loro hanno fondato la Sacra corona libera.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera is a splinter group founded by former SCU members.
edges.evidence_quote: La Sacra Corona Libera fu fondata nel 1998 da Massimo D'Amico, Antonio Vitale e Massimo Pasimeni, criminali mesagnesi già appartenuti alla Sacra corona unita.

descriptions: Società foggiana split from Sacra Corona Unita in 1986 due to conflicts with Giuseppe Rogoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel giro di pochi anni, Iannelli e Rizzi si liberarono da qualsiasi vincolo con la camorra cutoliana e aderirono alla Sacra Corona Unita, da cui però si staccarono nel 1986 a causa di contrasti con Giuseppe Rogoli, dando vita alla cosiddetta "Società foggiana".
